findings.title: Sampled-out access records still construct the full object access state
findings.impact: Every hot object access call now creates an ObjectAccessRecorder whose embedded ObjectAccessState contains many strings and std::function members, even when access_sample_rate drops the record immediately. Call sites such as KVClient::Get still instantiate the facade unconditionally before the sampler can return false, so sampled-out traffic avoids formatting but still pays repeated constructor/destructor and cache-footprint cost on the foreground request path.
findings.fix: Make the sampled-out facade genuinely lightweight: split the disabled recorder from the full state, store ObjectAccessState only behind a lazily constructed inline/optional state after shouldRecord_ is true, or add a cheap guard/factory that returns before building the full field state on dropped records.
